name: Cranberry Honey Mustard
recipeInstructions:
1. Combine the mustard seeds, cider vinegar, and dried cranberries in a non-aluminum pot or jar.
2. Cover and soak for 48 hours, adding additional vinegar as necessary to maintain enough liquid to cover the seeds.
3. Scrape the soaked seed-and-cranberry mixture into a food processor.
4. Process until the mixture turns from liquid and seeds to a creamy mixture flecked with seeds and bits of cranberry.
5. Add honey and salt.
6. Process for at least 3-4 minutes, adding additional vinegar as needed to achieve a nice creamy consistency.
7. The mustard will thicken slightly upon standing. It is ready to use immediately or may be aged for up to one week for improved flavor.
